title: ABI, Shared Libraries, and Foreign-Function Memory · C Memory Workbench
summary: A guided implementation pair receives a clean input trace and acceptance examples, then builds the first working versioned cross-language ownership ABI while narrating each size, lifetime, ownership, and cleanup decision. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of versioned cross-language ownership ABI while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Characterize calling convention, symbol visibility, integer widths, alignment, layout, and compiler flags for each supported ABI.
Expose stable opaque handles with versioned constructors, operations, observers, and same-library destruction.
Pass buffers with lengths, ownership direction, allocator family, and release callback across FFI.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Commit to the mission model · Workshop Abi Layout Call Contract
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop native-systems team handles memory case 1095. Produce bounded C evidence to validate a versioned foreign buffer and allocator family; change the size, lifetime, failure, or ownership condition; print the observed result and invariant. Instant code stays inside the PicoC practice subset. C23 syntax, current GCC and Clang, optimization, ABI, threads, sanitizers, analyzers, fuzzers, operating-system resources, foreign runtimes, load, and production behavior require named controlled transfer gates. Reject the unsupported conclusion, name the violated Wasm Linear Memory condition, repair the responsible layer, and rerun the boundary trace.
Keep the plausible output and hide the assumption to avoid confusing the stakeholder. That claim does not survive the test an acceptance boundary evidence boundary.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Treat WebAssembly pointers as offsets into bounded linear memory with copy, growth, lifetime, and host-reference rules.
